name: From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón
name_original: De Aldea del Rey a Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular por el Río Jabalón
description: Cycle 33.8 miles (54.4 km) on this moderate road cycling route from Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava, following the Río Jabalón.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by the plains of Campo de Calatrava, river valleys such as the Río Jabalón, and the distant hills of Sierra de Puertollano. The region offers a mix of open agricultural areas and routes connecting historical towns. Elevation changes are present, ranging from gentle undulations along riverbeds to more sustained climbs in the hiller sections. Best road cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey connecting several towns and rural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón, a moderate 33.8 miles (54.4 km) path. This route follows the Río Jabalón, providing views of the river and surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Bolaños, Torralba y Carrión de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro, a 27.9 miles (45.0 km) trail leading through the Campo de Calatrava, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Road cycling around Granátula De Calatrava is defined by the open plains of Campo de Calatrava, riverine landscapes along the Río Jabalón, and the rolling hills of the Sierra de Puertollano. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes to suit various ability levels. The routes in Granátula De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from around 18 reviews. More than 380 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Granátula De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Granátula De Calatrava?
A: There are 23 road cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: Granátula De Calatrava offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, including 5 easy, 13 moderate, and 5 difficult options, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are 5 easy road cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. One popular easy route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249647502/bolanos-torralba-y-carrion-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños, Torralba y Carrión de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.9 miles (45.0 km) long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Granátula De Calatrava?
A: The road cycling routes around Granátula De Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by the open plains of Campo de Calatrava, river valleys such as the Río Jabalón, and the distant rolling hills of Sierra de Puertollano. You'll find a mix of agricultural areas and routes connecting historical towns.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult road cycling routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246843298/sierra-de-puertollano-and-huertezuelas-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Puertollano and Huertezuelas de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 83.2 miles (133.9 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Granátula De Calatrava?
A: Many routes in the region are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251905687/pozuelo-de-calatrava-miguelturra-y-ermita-de-santa-marina-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251868469/campo-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: While specific data isn't provided, the region's climate suggests spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be very hot, especially in the open plains, so early mornings or late afternoons would be preferable during those months.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Granátula De Calatrava?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from around 18 reviews. Road cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the open plains to the riverine landscapes, and the network of routes connecting historical towns.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the Campo de Calatrava plains and the Río Jabalón. For specific natural attractions, consider exploring areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervideros Baños del Barranco&lt;/a&gt; or the volcanic landscapes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt;, which can be found near some routes.

Q: What interesting landmarks can I explore near the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history. Along or near the routes, you can find landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro)&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, offering cultural stops during your ride.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251905687/pozuelo-de-calatrava-miguelturra-y-ermita-de-santa-marina-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; takes about 3 hours 25 minutes, while the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249647502/bolanos-torralba-y-carrion-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños, Torralba y Carrión de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes.
tips:
This town is located in the valley of the Jabalón river, a tributary of the Guadiana on its left bank. In addition, its municipal term is part of the center of the province.
It is characterized by having a landscape with a great presence, especially in the cool mountains of the southwest with gall oaks, pine forests and streams, and in the west, in the area known as the valley, with the presence of rebollos, strawberry trees, heather. Therefore, we will find richness and natural diversity throughout its term.
In the urban nucleus is the Palacio de Clavería from the 16th century, home to the clavero, the person who guarded the keys to the Convent of Calatrava.
Other religious constructions are the Church of San Jorge Mártir and the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Valle. In the town center is the Palacio de Clavería from the 16th century, home to the clavier, the person who guarded the keys to the Calatrava Convent.
Other religious constructions are the Church of San Jorge Mártir and the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Valle.

In the heart ✨ of the Campo de Calatrava Volcanic Massif at an altitude between 750 and 850 meters above sea level, in the term of the noble and cultural city of Almagro, delimited by the Sierra de Arzollar, the Cañada Real Soriana, between valleys and mountains with a great density of holm oaks and junipers, rosemary, thyme, etc., is the Encomienda de Cervera Estate, where you can distinguish the black earths of volcanic origin, the red ones (Almagre) which, according to some historians, give their name from the city of Almagro, the limestone and stony. Highway CM-4111

The careful exterior appearance of this small hermitage is striking, rebuilt at the beginning of the 20th century and recently restored. Despite this, access to the small temple can only be done on foot. The path is practically lost and neglected between farmland and vegetation, so much so that reaching it on foot is practically cross-country. Source: Mancha Ignota

The parish church of San Juan Bautista is a temple from the 18th century, with a Latin cross and a single nave. As annexes it has a tower, attached to the foot of the church and sacristy, attached to the right side of the temple and to one of the arms of the transept. The roof is made of curved tiles, and the structure is made of masonry on the walls, ashlar on the buttresses and brick on the arches.

Of the interior it is necessary to emphasize the half orange dome on pendentives that finish off the transept. The structure is supported by four stone columns, two of them uncovered and two plastered.

In the heart of Campo de Calatrava is Valenzuela de Calatrava. It limits to the north, east and west with the city of Almagro, from which it is barely 6 kilometers away, and to the south with Granátula de Calatrava.
The origin of the town is in an Arab castle built by the Almoravids in the 12th century. and a small village dedicated to horticulture.
In Valenzuela de Calatrava, its gasified water fountains are famous both in public fountains and in houses.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate road ride. While it features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride, some segments might be unpaved or more challenging, requiring a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is a circular road cycling trail, starting and ending in the same general area, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route begins in Aldea del Rey. You can typically find parking within the town to start your ride. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but public parking should be available.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Río Jabalón?
faqs.answer: The route follows the Río Jabalón, offering picturesque views of the river and surrounding natural landscapes. You'll experience varied scenery typical of rural Spain, including natural areas and historical towns.

faqs.question: What are some notable places to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll pass through Aldea del Rey and Valenzuela de Calatrava. Other highlights include the historical site of Encomienda de Cervera, the Hermitage of Santa Marina, and the Church of San Juan Bautista.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: For road cycling in central Spain, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant weather conditions, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of official trails such as the Cañada Real Soriana Oriental, Cañada de Añavete, Camino de Bocapucheros, Camino del Puerto de Villafranca, and Palacio de Claveria. Castillo de Cva. la Nueva.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While road cycling routes are generally accessible, it's best to consider the safety of your dog on paved roads with potential traffic. If you bring a dog, ensure they are well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially near towns or on any unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through towns like Aldea del Rey and Valenzuela de Calatrava, you should find opportunities for cafes or shops to refuel and hydrate.

faqs.question: What should I wear for road cycling in central Spain?
faqs.answer: Dress in layers suitable for the season. Even on warm days, mornings can be cool. Always wear appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, padded shorts, and breathable clothing. Sun protection is crucial, especially during warmer months.
